name: I²C Bus
description: This component sets up the I²C bus for your ESP32, ESP8266, RP2040 or NRF52. In order for these components to work correctly, you need to define the I²C bus in your configuration. On ESP32 (both Arduino and ESP-IDF frameworks), internal pullup resistors are enabled by default. On ESP8266, the internal 10kΩ pullup resistors are always enabled. External pullups are still recommended for longer wire runs or multiple devices. You can use multiple devices on one I²C bus as each device is given a unique address for communicating between it and the ESP. You can do this by hopping wires from the two lines (SDA and SCL) from each device board to the next device board or by connecting the wires from each device back to the two I²C pins on the ESP.

config_entries.label: SCL
config_entries.description: The pin for the clock line of the I²C bus. Defaults to the default of your board (usually GPIO22 for ESP32 and GPIO5 for ESP8266).

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: SDA
config_entries.description: The pin for the data line of the I²C bus. Defaults to the default of your board (usually GPIO21 for ESP32 and GPIO4 for ESP8266).

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for this I²C bus if you need multiple I²C buses.

config_entries.label: Frequency
config_entries.description: Set the frequency the I²C bus should operate on. Defaults to `50kHz`. Default for NRF52 is `100kHz`. Values are `10kHz`, `50kHz`, `100kHz`, `200kHz`, ... `800kHz`. NRF52 supports only `100kHz` and `400kHz`.

config_entries.label: Low Power Mode
config_entries.description: Enable the low-power (master only) I²C bus. Only available on ESP32C5, ESP32C6 and ESP32P4. Defaults to `false` unless required.

config_entries.label: Scan
config_entries.description: If ESPHome should do a search of the I²C address space on startup. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: SCL Pullup Enabled
config_entries.description: Enable the internal pullup resistor for the SCL pin. Defaults to `true`. Only available on ESP32.

config_entries.label: SDA Pullup Enabled
config_entries.description: Enable the internal pullup resistor for the SDA pin. Defaults to `true`. Only available on ESP32.

config_entries.label: Timeout
config_entries.description: Set the I²C bus timeout. Defaults to the framework defaults (`100us` on `esp32`, `1s` on `esp8266` and `1s` on `rp2040`). Maximum on `esp32` is 13ms.
